title: crataegus × canescens
intro: Crataegus × canescens, commonly known as the hybrid hawthorn, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae and is a cross between Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata. The health benefits of Crataegus × canescens include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce cholesterol levels. The therapeutic actions of this plant are attributed to its ability to act as a vasodilator, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus × canescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Crataegus × canescens has many benefits, such as its ability to support heart health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, and improving overall cardiovascular function. Additionally, this plant is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect cells from damage and reduce inflammation, potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Crataegus × canescens has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gout, improving joint health and overall quality of life. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, leading to a longer and healthier life.
actions: Crataegus × canescens has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and stress in individuals. The flavonoids and polyphenols present in this plant have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease. Furthermore, the antiviral and antibacterial properties of Crataegus × canescens make it effective in treating infections and promoting wound healing, which can improve daily life by reducing the risk of illness and promoting overall well-being. By addressing these health issues, Crataegus × canescens can enhance the quality of life for individuals, allowing them to engage in physical activities, maintain a healthy lifestyle, and enjoy life to the fullest.
intro_study: According to "Natural product research", crataegus × canescens  may have benefits for cardiovascular diseases due to the presence of corosolic and euscapic acids, which have been reported to possess beneficial effects on cardiovascular diseases.
constituents: Crataegus × canescens has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective effects, thereby improving heart health and reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases. The plant also contains various triterpenoids like oleanolic acid, ursolic acid, and crataegusin, which possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. In addition, Crataegus × canescens contains various phenolic acids like caffeic acid, ferulic acid, and sinapic acid, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, thereby helping to prevent infections and promote overall well-being. By consuming Crataegus × canescens, individuals can benefit from improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, leading to a better quality of life and increased resilience against diseases.
parts: Crataegus × canescens has many medicinal parts, such as the berries, leaves, and flowers. The berries of Crataegus × canescens are rich in flavonoids, anthocyanins, and organic acids, which contribute to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The flowers of Crataegus × canescens are also a source of flavonoids and triterpenoids, and have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
preparations: Crataegus × canescens has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that are used for medicinal purposes. The tea made from the leaves and branches of Crataegus × canescens is used to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, while also exhibiting antioxidant properties. The tincture, which is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant, is often used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, due to its carminative properties. Additionally, the infusion of the plant's flowers is used to promote relaxation and reduce stress due to its sedative effects.
side_effects: Crataegus × canescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues, and interactions with medications. For instance, consuming large quantities of the plant's fruit or leaves can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, using Crataegus × canescens in combination with blood thinners or diabetes medications can increase the risk of bleeding or low blood sugar. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can also lead to kidney damage or liver toxicity.
